channel.title: Ad News Feed
channel.description: 広告プラットフォーム最新情報の自動収集フィード

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Play smarter on the world's biggest stage for football
item.description: Learn how Microsoft Advertising helps brands engage fans, stay visible across screens, and convert intent during this summer's biggest football tournament.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads Conversion Tracking: Master Mobile Clicks Setup #shorts
item.description: Master Google Ads conversion tracking with Tag Manager. Learn to set up mobile clicks to call, manage conversions, and enhance data insights for better ad performance. #GoogleAds #TagManager #ConversionTracking #DigitalMarketing #AdOptimization

item.title: [Google Ads] A more personal digital health experience for people in Europe
item.description: Google and DocMorris have announced a partnership to create a more intuitive and supportive digital health experience.

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Ads for Restricted Industries: 3 Strategies to Win [Inside Google Ads Episode 112]
item.description: If you work in a so-called "Sensitive Interest Category" like healthcare, finance, legal, or real estate, you’ve likely stared at that "Eligible (Limited)" status in Google Ads with frustration. Operating in a sensitive interest category means that standard Google Ads tactics like retargeting or bas

item.title: [Google Ads] Google Keyword Planner: Your Secret Budgeting Tool Revealed! #shorts
item.description: Unlock accurate search campaign budget forecasts. Learn to leverage Google Keyword Planner and understand industry spending for smarter ad investments. #SearchAds #Budgeting #MarketingTips #GoogleAds #Forecasting

item.title: [Google Ads] AI shopping gets simpler with Universal Commerce Protocol updates
item.description: Universal Commerce Protocol (UCP) releases new capabilities, and Google shares a new onboarding experience to simplify UCP integration.

item.title: [Meta Ads] Boosting Your Support and Safety on Meta’s Apps With AI
[General] AI-Related Ad Account Shutdowns, Meta AI Chat, and More
[General] Content Decay: What It Is, Why It Happens, and How to Fix It Before You Lose 80% of Your Traffic
item.description: Every piece of content you have ever published is in a slow competition with time, competitors, and shifting search behavior. That article ranking #2 for your best keyword two years ago? It may be hemorrhaging traffic right now — and you might not even know it. This is not a hypothetical. It happ

item.title: [General] Shopify SEO: The Data-Backed Guide to Ranking Your Store on Google and AI Search (2026)
item.description: Shopify SEO is the practice of optimizing your Shopify store so that it appears prominently in search engine results pages (SERPs) for queries your potential customers are already typing. It encompasses everything from how your site is structured and how fast it loads to the words on your product

item.title: [General] AI in Software Development in 2026: Verified Productivity Data, Leading Tools, and the Risks Most Teams Overlook
item.description: There is a gap between how AI in software development is discussed and what it actually does on the ground. Vendor claims lean toward the optimistic end of the spectrum. Developer forums lean toward skepticism. The reality sits somewhere in between, and the evidence is now detailed enough to make

item.title: [General] Nike x Beats Powerbeats Pro 2 Special Edition: First-Ever Hardware Collab, LeBron James Campaign, Full Specs, Price & Where to Buy (2026)
item.description: March 17, 2026 is the day two of the most recognizable logos in sports — the Nike Swoosh and the Beats “b” — appeared on the same piece of hardware for the first time in their shared history. The product at the center of it all is the Powerbeats Pro 2 – Nike Special Edition: a […

item.title: [General] Substack for Digital Marketers: The Complete Channel Guide Every Marketer Needs in 2026
item.description: Not every platform that grows fast deserves a place in your marketing strategy. But when a publishing platform hits 47.6 million unique monthly website visitors — up 65.85% in a single year — and its own CEO is talking publicly about a path from 5 million to 50 million paid subscribers, it earns

item.title: [General] How to Use Discord for Digital PR and Brand Engagement: The Complete Marketer’s Playbook for Earned Media
item.description: Most PR teams are working harder for fewer results. According to data from Propel, journalists responded to fewer than 3.15% of pitches in a recent quarter — and that number has been declining year over year. Meanwhile, Facebook’s organic reach has fallen to between 1% and 2% for most brand

item.title: [General] Local Landing Pages That Convert: The Complete Step-by-Step Guide to Building Pages That Rank and Generate Leads
item.description: Every day, millions of people open their phones and type something like “emergency plumber in Dallas,” “best dentist near me,” or “HVAC repair in Tampa.” They are not browsing. They are ready to act. If your business does not have a dedicated, well-built local

item.title: [General] How to Create a Wikipedia Page for Your Business in 2026: 10 Proven Steps That Actually Get Approved
item.description: There is a reason seasoned marketers and PR professionals treat a Wikipedia presence as one of the most coveted milestones in a brand’s digital footprint. Wikipedia is not just an encyclopedia — it is the fifth most visited website on the internet, pulling in nearly 2 billion unique visitor

item.title: [General] Reddit Keyword Research: 4 Methods to Find Keywords Your Competitors Are Missing (2026 Guide)
item.description: Search Google for almost any product recommendation, how-to question, or buying decision — and you will find Reddit threads sitting in the top results. This is not a coincidence. Google has deepened its indexing of Reddit content substantially, and as of 2026, Reddit threads regularly appear in t

item.title: [General] Landing Page Optimization: 12 Data-Backed Strategies That Increase Conversions (2026 Guide)
item.description: Most landing pages are leaking conversions. Not because the offer is wrong, but because the page itself is getting in the way. Across an analysis of more than 41,000 landing pages and 464 million visits, the median landing page conversion rate sits at just 6.6%. The top 10% of performers, however

item.title: [Google Ads] 200+ Clicks: Unlock Google Ads Success & Conversions! #shorts
item.description: Unlock optimal Google Ads results. A minimum of 200 monthly clicks is key to gathering enough conversion data for effective optimization and improved ROI. #GoogleAds #DigitalMarketing #AdvertisingTips #PerformanceMarketing

item.title: [Meta Ads] What Do Most Advertisers Get Wrong About Meta Ads?
item.description: Today's question is about the single most common point of confusion Jon wishes everyone understood about Meta ads. He can't pick just one, so he gives three. Jon explains why the algorithm being literal shapes everything, why targeting control is mostly unnecessary now, and where advertisers should

item.title: [Meta Ads] Creator Fast Track: A New Way to Quickly Grow Your Audience and Earn Money on Facebook
[General] Keyword Search Volume: The Complete 2026 Guide to Finding, Analyzing, and Using Data That Actually Drives Rankings
item.description: There is a persistent belief in SEO that chasing the highest-volume keywords is the fastest route to organic traffic. It is not. In fact, it is one of the most reliably expensive mistakes a business can make with its content budget. Keyword search volume is one of the most referenced metrics in d

item.title: [General] 17 Best Free SEO Courses and Certifications in 2026 (Beginner to Advanced)
item.description: Organic search accounts for roughly 53% of all website traffic, according to data compiled across multiple industry studies. Despite that, the majority of businesses still underinvest in search engine optimization simply because the learning curve feels expensive. The assumption that quality SEO

item.title: [General] AI Search Trends 2026: The Data, Shifts, and Strategies Marketers Need Right Now
item.description: Search did not gradually evolve in 2026. It restructured. Within a single calendar year, the way people find information, compare products, make purchasing decisions, and engage with brands shifted in ways that have been building for years but arrived faster than most marketing teams were prepare

item.title: [General] Agentic Commerce Optimization (ACO): The Complete Guide to Getting Your Products Recommended by AI in 2026
item.description: If you run an ecommerce business or manage digital marketing for one, you have probably noticed that something significant is happening to organic traffic. Search Engine Journal reported in early 2026 that traditional Google traffic is down 20–50% across broad retail categories, with fashion and

item.title: [General] L’Oréal Paris x The Devil Wears Prada 2: Inside the Oscars Ad Featuring Kendall Jenner and Simone Ashley — and What It Means for Beauty Marketing in 2026
item.description: On March 15, 2026, during the 98th Annual Academy Awards broadcast, L’Oréal Paris stepped into the Runway Magazine offices — not as a brand interrupting entertainment, but as a character within it. The world’s number one beauty brand launched a custom ad spot during one of television&

item.title: [General] McDonald’s CARDS UK: The Complete Guide to All 25 Collectible Cards, the £4 Million Prize Pool, and Why Ronald McDonald Is Back
item.description: There is a quiet but significant shift happening inside X, the platform formerly known as Twitter, and it is worth paying close attention to—especially if you manage a brand, run paid campaigns, or create content professionally. The platform is currently testing a new prompt that would alert user

item.title: [General] Instagram Clickable Links in Post Captions Are Finally Being Tested — Here’s the Complete Breakdown for Creators, Brands, and Marketers
item.description: For over a decade, anyone who has spent time building an audience on Instagram has run into the same wall: you cannot place a clickable link inside a post caption. It does not matter how many followers you have, how high your engagement rate is, or how useful that link would be to your audience.

item.title: [General] X (Formerly Twitter) Is Testing AI Content Alerts Before You Share: What It Means for Brands, Creators, and Platform Trust

The platform is currently testing a new prompt that would alert user

item.title: [General] Meta Replaces “Sponsored” with “Ad” on Instagram and Facebook: What Every Advertiser Needs to Know
item.description: Meta is in the middle of one of its quietest but most consequential shifts in how it communicates paid content to its users. Across Instagram and, now in limited testing, Facebook, the familiar gray word “Sponsored” that has sat beneath advertiser handles for over a decade is being re

item.title: [General] Reddit Partners With Pacvue: What It Means for Advertisers, Retail Media, and Brand Discovery in 2026
item.description: Advertising on Reddit has long carried an asterisk. The platform’s communities are among the most engaged and intellectually active on the internet, but the friction of managing Reddit campaigns alongside broader retail media investments made it easy to deprioritize. That is changing. On Ma

item.description: Master monthly Google Ads budgeting using weekly insights and keyword data. Aim for approximately $3,200 for peak performance and optimal results. #GoogleAds #PPC #DigitalMarketing #AdvertisingTips

item.title: [Google Ads] Non Skippable Video Reach Campaigns
item.description:
In March of 2026, Google Ads announced that non-skippable Video Reach campaigns are available globally in Google Ads and 360. Because of this change, we wanted to make sure all advertisers know how to set up this campaign, and what the main goals of this campaign should be.
0:30 - Creating a Non-S

item.title: [Meta Ads] When Weird Stuff Happens to Meta Ads
[General] How AI Is Impacting Local Search: Data, Facts, and What Every Business Must Do in 2026
item.description: There’s a moment every local business owner eventually has: you search for your own business on Google, and the top of the page no longer looks the way you remember it. A large grey box now sits above the familiar blue links. It might mention your business. It might not. It might get a deta

item.title: [General] 25+ Best Conferences for Digital Agencies to Attend in the USA in 2026 (Dates, Costs & What to Expect)
item.description: The digital marketing landscape in 2026 is moving at a pace that makes it genuinely difficult to keep up from behind a screen. Global digital advertising spend is projected to surpass $1 trillion for the first time this year, with nearly 69% of that total flowing into digital channels. Artificial

item.title: [General] Starbucks “The Magic of Coffee” Ad (2026): Inside the David Gelb Campaign, the Craft Strategy Behind It, and What Every Brand Can Learn
item.description: There is a moment in Starbucks’ newest ad where nothing happens except coffee. No celebrity. No coupon code. No limited-time-offer countdown clock. Just coffee cherries being handpicked on a hillside, barista hands tamping finely ground espresso, and the quiet pull of a shot filling a cup.

item.title: [General] Google Maps Gets a New Icon and Its Biggest AI Upgrade in a Decade: Ask Maps, Immersive Navigation, and What It Means for Your Business (2026)
item.description: In early March 2026, Google Maps quietly rolled out a redesigned app icon. There was no press release, no keynote, no staged reveal. The update simply arrived in the App Store and Google Play as part of a version update, and users began noticing the difference on their home screens. That icon cha

item.title: [General] UGG Chose Handcraft Over AI for Its Fluff Yeah Comeback — Here’s What the Brand’s B-Movie Campaign Reveals About Marketing in 2026
item.description: In a spring 2026 advertising season already crowded with algorithmically generated imagery and AI-voiced copy, UGG made a different bet. The Southern California footwear brand mounted a miniature city of Los Angeles — built from wood and paper over four weeks — put a comedian inside it, and let t

item.title: [General] xAI Loses 9 of 11 Co-Founders: What the Exodus Means for the SpaceX IPO, Grok, and Elon Musk’s $1.25 Trillion Rebuild
item.description: In fewer than six weeks since Elon Musk completed the largest corporate merger in history — combining SpaceX and xAI at a combined valuation of $1.25 trillion — the artificial intelligence company he founded in 2023 has shed nine of its eleven original co-founders. The most recent departures, Zih

item.title: [General] How Celsius Holdings Captured 20% of the U.S. Energy Drink Market by Building Brands, Not Just Beverages
item.description: The U.S. energy drink market has a new second-largest player. Celsius Holdings — the parent company behind the Celsius, Alani Nu, and Rockstar Energy brands — reported full-year 2025 revenue of $2.5 billion, representing year-over-year growth of more than 85%. Its combined portfolio commanded app

item.title: [General] Burger King Fired Its Mascot and Handed the Crown to Its Customers — Here’s Exactly What That Means for Brand Strategy
item.description: There are moments in marketing when a brand stops performing and starts telling the truth. On the night of March 15, 2026, during the Academy Awards broadcast watched by nearly 20 million Americans, Burger King had one of those moments. A 90-second ad aired that did something most large fast-food

item.title: [General] Meta’s New Original Content Rules (2026): What Every Creator on Facebook and Instagram Must Know
item.description: If you create content on Facebook or Instagram — whether you’re a solo creator, a brand page manager, or a social media professional managing multiple clients — March 2026 marks a line in the sand. Meta has formally updated its content guidelines, launched new enforcement tools, and publish

item.title: [General] Google Runs Hundreds of Crawlers Most SEOs Have Never Heard Of — Here’s What That Means for Your Site
item.description: The name “Googlebot” has been part of every SEO conversation for more than two decades. item.title: [Meta Ads] Click Attribution Isn't What It Used to Be
item.description: Meta changed how click attribution works, and it's mostly for the better, but some advertisers might see a negative impact. [Meta Ads] Transitioning from Nielsen DMA to Comscore Markets for automotive offer ads
item.description: Beginning on June 22, 2026, we will discontinue the use of Nielsen’s Designated Market Area (DMA) for automotive model ads.

This update provides developers and advertisers with unprecedented transparency into where the

item.title: [Google Ads] Announcing v23 of the Google Ads API

item.title: [Google Ads] Campaign Manager 360 API v4 Sunset Reminder
item.description:
Campaign Manager 360 API v4 will be sunset on February 26, 2026. Starting on this date, all v4 API requests will begin to fail. If you’re still using this version, we strongly encourage you to migrate to v5 as soon as possible to avoid service interruptions.

See the

item.title: [Google Ads] Update your PAL integration for new limited ads behavior
item.description:
The Programmatic Access Library (PAL) is updating user privacy and data usage settings, and requires implementation updates as these changes go live. This update introduces the following changes:

item.title: [Google Ads] Explorer access is now available for Google Ads API developers
item.description: We are rolling out a new Explorer Access level access tier for Google Ads API over the next couple of weeks. This will allow new developers signing up for Google Ads API to gain immediate access to production

item.title: [Google Ads] October 2025 update to Display & Video 360 API
item.description:
Today, we’re announcing the October 2025 update to the Display & Video 360 API. This update includes the following:

  • The ability to create and retrieve

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Decoding Ramadan 2026: Data-Driven Strategies for the Middle East, Türkiye, and Africa
item.description: Microsoft Advertising and InMobi Advertising highlight key Ramadan consumer and search trends in the Middle East, Türkiye, and Africa, outlining marketing

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] AI web and zero UI: What your brand should do when the web stops asking you to click
item.description: What happens when the web stops asking you to click? Learn how the AI Web and zero UI are reshaping discovery, action, and what brands must do next.

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Advanced Consent Mode: Preserving accurate measurement while respecting user privacy
item.description: Advanced Consent Mode can help you respect user privacy choices, maintain trust, and provide the insights needed to measure and boost performance.

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Build real-world Performance Max expertise with the new Microsoft Advertising learning path
item.description: Get practical training on Microsoft Advertising Performance Max in our new learning path, from setup to optimization—plus earn a sharable badge.

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Ad preview hub and other product news for February 2026
item.description: See what’s new in Microsoft Advertising for February 2026: an enhanced ad preview hub for Audience ads, plus Performance Max and Google import updates.

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Celebrating A Year of Achievement and Excellence: Microsoft Advertising Partner Program 2026
item.description: Join us as we take a look at some of the Microsoft Advertising Partner Program’s most notable events of 2025 and explore exciting changes for 2026.

item.title: [Microsoft Ads] Understanding AI search: A guide for modern marketers
item.description: The updated AI Marketer’s Guide is a practical blueprint that shows how AI‑powered search works and the actions marketers can take to drive results.
